questions.text: What does 'flow of program' refer to?
questions.explanation: The flow of a program describes the sequence in which statements are executed. By default programs run sequentially unless control structures like conditions or loops modify the execution.

choices.text: The order in which statements execute
The memory used by a program
The number of variables in a program

questions.text: Which is the default execution flow of any program?
questions.explanation: Programs normally execute sequentially from top to bottom unless altered by conditional or looping statements.

questions.text: Which structure is used to make decisions in a program?
questions.explanation: Conditional statements like if or switch allow a program to execute different code depending on a condition.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a control flow mechanism?
questions.explanation: Control flow mechanisms include conditionals and loops. Variables only store data and do not control execution.

questions.text: What is a flowchart?
questions.explanation: A flowchart is a graphical representation of an algorithm using standard symbols to visualize the program logic.

questions.text: Which shape represents a process in a flowchart?
questions.explanation: A rectangle represents a process or instruction step in a flowchart.

questions.text: Which shape represents a decision in a flowchart?
questions.explanation: A diamond is used to represent a decision point where the flow branches depending on a condition.

questions.text: Which shape represents the start or end of a program in a flowchart?
questions.explanation: An oval (terminator symbol) is used to indicate the start or end of the program flow.

questions.text: What is pseudocode?
questions.explanation: Pseudocode is an informal way of writing algorithms using plain language that resembles programming logic.

choices.text: A human-readable description of algorithm steps

questions.text: Which keyword is used to terminate a loop early?
questions.explanation: The break statement exits the loop immediately and continues execution after the loop.

questions.text: Which keyword skips the remaining code in the current loop iteration?
questions.explanation: Continue skips the current iteration and proceeds with the next iteration of the loop.

questions.text: Which loop is generally used when the number of iterations is known?
questions.explanation: A for loop is commonly used when the iteration count is known beforehand.
range(3) generates numbers 0, 1, and 2.

questions.text: What is an infinite loop?
questions.explanation: An infinite loop continues forever because the terminating condition never becomes false.

questions.text: Which statement does nothing but is syntactically required?
questions.explanation: The pass statement acts as a placeholder when no operation is needed.

questions.text: Nested conditions refer to:
questions.explanation: Nested conditions occur when one if statement is placed inside another.

choices.text: An if statement inside another if

questions.text: What is the main advantage of flowcharts?
questions.explanation: Flowcharts make algorithms easier to visualize and understand before writing code.

questions.text: Arrows in a flowchart represent:
questions.explanation: Arrows indicate the direction of control flow between steps.

questions.text: Control flow statements help to:
questions.explanation: They determine the order in which program instructions are executed.

questions.text: What happens if an if condition is false and no else exists?
questions.explanation: The program simply skips the if block and moves to the next statement.

questions.text: Explain what is meant by the flow of a program.
questions.explanation: Students should explain how instructions are executed in a specific order and how control structures modify this sequence.

questions.text: What are flowcharts and why are they useful?
questions.explanation: Flowcharts visually represent algorithms using standardized symbols which help in understanding program logic.

questions.text: Explain pseudocode and its advantages.
questions.explanation: Pseudocode is a structured description of an algorithm using simple language that helps plan program logic.

questions.text: Describe the difference between sequential flow and conditional flow.
questions.explanation: Sequential flow executes statements in order while conditional flow chooses execution paths based on conditions.

questions.text: Explain how loops change the flow of a program.
questions.explanation: Loops allow repeated execution of code blocks until a condition becomes false.

questions.explanation: A leap year is divisible by 4 but not by 100 unless it is also divisible by 400.
